site_2193b646.name: Apex Array
site_2193b646.description: Three obsidian-black spires, each twice the height of a person, arranged in a tight triangle. Their surfaces are perfectly smooth, occasionally shimmering with internal data streams visible as brief, geometric patterns of cyan light.
site_2193b646.identity: A cluster of three monolithic data spires used for high-altitude signal collection and transmission.

site_ac921010.name: Calibration Ring
site_ac921010.description: A disc of brushed metal, ten meters in diameter, flush with the basalt. A complex circuit pattern is etched into its surface, and it emits a constant, low hum that vibrates through the soles of your boots.
site_ac921010.identity: A flat, circular platform of advanced alloy used for precision instrument alignment and diagnostic routines.

M4x1:site_start.name: Survey Vault
M4x1:site_start.description: A hexagonal hatch, forged from a single piece of dull grey alloy, set directly into the mountain. A single, softly pulsing white glyph at its center serves as both lock and status indicator.
M4x1:site_start.identity: A sealed, subterranean structure housing autonomous survey drones and geological analysis cores.

M4x1:site_start/l2.name: Survey Vault
M4x1:site_start/l2.type: A sealed, subterranean structure housing autonomous survey drones and geological analysis cores.

turn_history.narrative:
You tap the sleek, matte-black module on your chest, and a soft, ascending chime confirms the flight recorder is active. A faint, translucent HUD flickers into existence at the periphery of your vision, displaying a clean readout of your biometrics and a topographical map of the peak.

The air is thin and cold, scoured clean by the high altitude. You stand on a broad, flat expanse of dark, polished basalt, like a natural landing pad. To the west, the mountain falls away into a treacherous slope of loose **scree**. The other directions offer only more of the same austere summit.

Three distinct sites punctuate the stark landscape. A short distance to your left, a trio of monolithic, obsidian-black data spires—the **Apex Array**—stands silent, their surfaces occasionally glitching with a ripple of blue light. Ahead, a low, circular platform of brushed metal, the **Calibration Ring**, hums with a sub-audible frequency you feel in your teeth. And directly before you, set into the rock face, is a seamless, hexagonal hatch marked with a single pulsing glyph—the entrance to the **Survey Vault**.

The recorder logs your position, the atmospheric pressure, and the ambient energy signatures emanating from the three silent sites.
